Setup and Data Loading

I use Alphavantage site for stock monthly information API query: and the Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) for of all carriers’ monthly delay data.

The fuel data is from Fred

Import Data

delay_data<- read_csv(here("data", "delay_data.csv")) 

DAL<- read_csv(here("data","DAL_month.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Carrier= "DAL")
UAL<- read_csv(here("data","UAL_month.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Carrier= "UAL")
JBLU<- read_csv(here("data","JBLU_month.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Carrier= "JBLU")
AAL<- read_csv(here("data","AAL_month.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Carrier= "AAL")
ALK<- read_csv(here("data","ALK_month.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Carrier= "ALK")
HA<- read_csv(here("data","HA_month.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Carrier= "HA")
ULCC<- read_csv(here("data","ULCC_month.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Carrier= "ULCC")
LUV<- read_csv(here("data","LUV_month.csv")) %>%
  mutate(Carrier= "LUV")

carrier_names<-  read_csv(here("data","Airline_Delay_Cause_26.csv")) %>% 
  dplyr::select(carrier,carrier_name) %>%
  distinct(carrier, .keep_all = TRUE) %>% 
  arrange(carrier)

Data Handling

Data Cleaning

all_stock<- bind_rows(DAL,UAL,JBLU,AAL,ALK, HA, ULCC,LUV) %>% 
  rename(S_Date= timestamp) %>% 
  mutate(month= month(S_Date),
         M_day= day(S_Date),
         year= year(S_Date)) 

rm(DAL,UAL,JBLU,AAL,ALK, HA, ULCC,LUV)

Combine all Data

The dependent variable “grow_log” captures intra-month price movement, rather than a traditional month-over-month return. This reduces noise from carry-over effects between months, at the cost of not capturing the full calendar-month return investors experience.

I use \(\frac{close}{open}\), which will later be used in log model, due to the advantages of a logarithmic model in predicting percentage change.

Data dates panel:

## # A tibble: 8 × 4
##   carrier Start      End        Observations

## 5 JBLU    2003-06-30 2025-10-22          269
## 6 LUV     2003-06-30 2025-10-22          269
## 7 UAL     2006-03-31 2025-10-22          236
## 8 ULCC    2021-05-28 2025-10-22           54

Correlation Graphs

here is a correlation table of the main numeric variables. some have obvious correlation like the open and close of the same date and firm.

av_delay_df %>%
  mutate(month= as.factor(month)) %>% 
  select(8:11,14,19) %>%

Data Density

Stock value at close over time and the effect of COVID-19

Predicting grow by year with month and carrier effect

feols(
  grow ~ year | Carrier + factor(month),
  data = (all_stock %>% 
            mutate(grow= close/open)),
  cluster = ~ Carrier)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: grow
## Observations: 1,954
## Fixed-effects: Carrier: 8,  factor(month): 12
## Standard-errors: Clustered (Carrier) 
##      Estimate Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|) 
## year -1.5e-05   0.000221 -0.068495  0.94731 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.15097     Adj. R2: 0.019468
##                 Within R2: 4.376e-7

Month effect

delay_data %>%
select(airport,carrier,year, month, arr_delay, arr_flights) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(
    arr_delay = sum(arr_delay, na.rm = TRUE),
    arr_flights = sum(arr_flights, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%  
  mutate(av_delay= arr_delay/arr_flights) %>% 
  select(-arr_delay,-arr_flights)
## # A tibble: 12 × 2
##    month av_delay
##    <dbl>    <dbl>
##  1     1    10.8 
##  2     2    10.4 
##  3     3    10.6 
##  4     4    10.6 
##  5     5    11.7 
##  6     6    15.0 
##  7     7    15.5 
##  8     8    12.8 
##  9     9     8.34
## 10    10     8.50
## 11    11     8.37
## 12    12    12.5

stock growth by month

Average stock grow by month and year as pixel graph

Average delay over time and the COVID-19 effect

We can see that the 2020 change not only the delays, but also the variance of the delays.

Average fuel price as a predictor of the stock growth

It seems like there is no clear correlation, at least not without variables handling

LM by carrier

av_delay_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x= av_delay, y= grow, color= carrier))+
  geom_point(alpha= 0.6)+
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se= F, color= "Black")+
    labs(
    title = "LM model by carrier",
    x = "delay",
    y = "growth",
    color = "Carrier"
  )+facet_wrap(~carrier)

lm_carrier_table <- bind_rows(
  lapply(
    split(av_delay_df, av_delay_df$carrier),
    function(df) {
      model <- lm(grow ~ av_delay, data = df)
      s <- summary(model)
      
      data.frame(
        Carrier   = unique(df$carrier),
        Intercept = round(coef(model)[1], 4),
        av_delay  = round(coef(model)[2], 4),
        R2        = round(s$r.squared, 4),
        Adj_R2    = round(s$adj.r.squared, 4),
        p_value   = round(s$coefficients[2, "Pr(>|t|)"], 4),
        N         = nrow(df), row.names = unique(df$carrier)
      )
    }
  )
)
lm_carrier_table
##      Carrier Intercept av_delay     R2  Adj_R2 p_value   N
## AAL      AAL    0.0584  -0.0035 0.0108  0.0066  0.1083 241
## ALK      ALK    0.0117  -0.0003 0.0001 -0.0036  0.8580 269
## DAL      DAL    0.0201  -0.0009 0.0008 -0.0037  0.6688 221
## HA        HA    0.0495  -0.0051 0.0059  0.0019  0.2257 250
## JBLU    JBLU    0.0282  -0.0017 0.0087  0.0050  0.1277 269
## LUV      LUV    0.0253  -0.0021 0.0067  0.0030  0.1792 269
## UAL      UAL    0.0616  -0.0037 0.0118  0.0076  0.0955 236
## ULCC    ULCC    0.1032  -0.0052 0.0210  0.0022  0.2959  54
#lm_carrier_table %>% write_csv(here("data","Carrier_pred.csv"))
long_df <- av_delay_df %>%
  select(S_Date, Fuel_price, close) %>%
  mutate(Fuel_price= Fuel_price) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Fuel_price, close),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "close")

Fuel price and stocks growth waves over time

### Distribution of monthly stock returns

ggplot(av_delay_df, aes(x = grow_log,fill= carrier)) +
  geom_density(bins = 40,alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~carrier, nrow= 2)+
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Monthly Stock Returns",
    x = "Monthly return (%)",
    y = "Count"
  )

mean_df <- av_delay_df %>%
  group_by(carrier) %>%
  summarise(mean_delay = mean(av_delay, na.rm = TRUE),
            xmin = min(av_delay, na.rm = TRUE),
            xmax = max(av_delay, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  mutate(label = paste0("\u03BC = ", round(mean_delay, 1)),
          x_mid = (xmin + xmax) / 2)

#mean_df %>% write_csv(here("data","late_mid_av.csv"))

ggplot(av_delay_df, aes(x = av_delay, fill = carrier)) +
  geom_density(adjust = 1/2, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~carrier, nrow = 2) +
  geom_vline(data = mean_df, aes(xintercept = mean_delay),
    color = "blue3",
    linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Average Delay by Carrier",
    x = "Delay (mean)") +
  theme_minimal()+
geom_text(
  data = mean_df,
  aes(x = x_mid, y = Inf, label = label),
  vjust = 2,
  hjust = 0.5,
  color = "red")

delay_long<-
  delay_data %>% 
  select(carrier, year, month,
         carrier_delay, weather_delay, nas_delay,
         security_delay, late_aircraft_delay) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(carrier_delay, weather_delay, nas_delay,
         security_delay, late_aircraft_delay),
    names_to = "Reason",
    values_to = "Delay_Min"
  ) %>% 
    mutate(
    Reason = gsub("_delay$", "", Reason, ignore.case = TRUE)
  )

delay_share <- delay_long %>%
  group_by(carrier, Reason) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Delay = sum(Delay_Min, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(carrier) %>%
  mutate(
    Share = Total_Delay / sum(Total_Delay)
  )

Delay composition by airline

## Saving 7 x 5 in image

Delay composition over time across all carriers

Modeling

Main Model

glm_stock_simple <- glm(log(grow_log) ~ av_delay + factor(month) + carrier,
                        data = av_delay_df,
                        family = gaussian())
summary(glm_stock_simple)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = log(grow_log) ~ av_delay + factor(month) + carrier, 
##     family = gaussian(), data = av_delay_df)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept)      0.0203069  0.0183529   1.106  0.26867   
## av_delay        -0.0020566  0.0007943  -2.589  0.00970 **
## factor(month)2  -0.0135002  0.0170472  -0.792  0.42851   
## factor(month)3  -0.0381136  0.0170188  -2.240  0.02525 * 
## factor(month)4  -0.0146255  0.0170240  -0.859  0.39039   
## factor(month)5  -0.0038630  0.0169921  -0.227  0.82019   
## factor(month)6  -0.0181844  0.0170451  -1.067  0.28619   
## factor(month)7   0.0188835  0.0171110   1.104  0.26992   
## factor(month)8   0.0031354  0.0169484   0.185  0.85325   
## factor(month)9  -0.0088947  0.0170091  -0.523  0.60108   
## factor(month)10  0.0336752  0.0169937   1.982  0.04767 * 
## factor(month)11  0.0444104  0.0171902   2.583  0.00986 **
## factor(month)12  0.0273966  0.0171040   1.602  0.10938   
## carrierALK      -0.0038987  0.0136079  -0.287  0.77452   
## carrierDAL      -0.0003984  0.0140182  -0.028  0.97733   
## carrierHA       -0.0041108  0.0151219  -0.272  0.78577   
## carrierJBLU      0.0014089  0.0131190   0.107  0.91449   
## carrierLUV      -0.0021809  0.0134643  -0.162  0.87135   
## carrierUAL       0.0051124  0.0134598   0.380  0.70412   
## carrierULCC     -0.0097959  0.0230647  -0.425  0.67110   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.02150465)
## 
##     Null deviance: 39.781  on 1808  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 38.472  on 1789  degrees of freedom
## AIC: -1790
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
glm_stock_simple %>% plot()

pred_simple <- tibble::tibble(
  prediction = exp(fitted(glm_stock_simple)),
  growth     = model.response(model.frame(glm_stock_simple)),
  Carrier    = model.frame(glm_stock_simple)$carrier,
  Date       = glm_stock_simple$data$S_Date
) |>
  mutate(
    pred_delta = growth - prediction
  )

pred_simple |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(prediction, growth),
               names_to = "type",
               values_to = "value")%>% 
ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = value, color = type)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ Carrier, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted vs Actual Growth Over Time",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Growth"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

pred_simple |>
ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = pred_delta)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ Carrier, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Unpredicted Actual Growth Over Time",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Unpredicted Growth"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

Main Model’s Stability Test

lmtest::bptest(glm_stock_simple)
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  glm_stock_simple
## BP = 63.009, df = 19, p-value = 1.281e-06

A Breusch-Pagan test strongly rejected the null hypothesis of homoskedasticity (BP = 63.01, p < 0.001), indicating the presence of heteroskedasticity. Therefore, heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors were employed in all subsequent regression.

car::vif(glm_stock_simple)
##                   GVIF Df GVIF^(1/(2*Df))
## av_delay      2.048449  1        1.431240
## factor(month) 1.213267 11        1.008826
## carrier       1.835900  7        1.044351

Variance Inflation Factors (VIF) were computed to assess multicollinearity. All values were below 5(even 2), indicating no severe multicollinearity concerns.

Robustness Checks

To verify the validity of the empirical results, several robustness checks were conducted. A Breusch–Pagan test indicated the presence of heteroskedasticity; therefore, all models were estimated using heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors. In addition, given the panel structure of the data (airlines observed over time), standard errors were clustered at the airline level to account for potential within-firm serial correlation.

Furthermore, alternative model specifications were estimated to test the stability of the main findings. In particular, a lagged delay specification was implemented to examine whether stock returns respond to operational performance with a temporal delay. The estimated coefficients remain stable in sign and magnitude across specifications, supporting the robustness of the central empirical results.

Fix Main Model

Pooled OLS may confound cross-sectional differences with within-firm effects; therefore a fixed-effects estimator is employed.

model_stock_fixest <- feols(
  log(grow_log) ~ av_delay | carrier + month,
  data = av_delay_df,
  cluster = ~ carrier
)

summary(model_stock_fixest)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(grow_log)
## Observations: 1,809
## Fixed-effects: carrier: 8,  month: 12
## Standard-errors: Clustered (carrier) 
##           Estimate Std. Error  t value Pr(>|t|)    
## av_delay -0.002057   0.000564 -3.64778 0.008202 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.145832     Adj. R2: 0.022638
##                  Within R2: 0.003733

Alternative Models

Fuel Effect

model_fuel_fixest <- feols(
  log(grow_log) ~ av_delay + fuel_del| carrier + month,
  data = av_delay_df,
  cluster = ~ carrier
)

summary(model_fuel_fixest)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(grow_log)
## Observations: 1,809
## Fixed-effects: carrier: 8,  month: 12
## Standard-errors: Clustered (carrier) 
##           Estimate Std. Error   t value  Pr(>|t|)    
## av_delay -0.002058   0.000568 -3.626450 0.0084375 ** 
## fuel_del -0.004181   0.031164 -0.134152 0.8970581    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.145831     Adj. R2: 0.022099
##                  Within R2: 0.00374

On Time Instead of Late

av_delay_df_on_time<-
  delay_data %>% 
  select(airport,carrier,year, month, arr_delay, arr_flights, arr_del15, arr_cancelled, arr_diverted) %>%
  group_by(carrier, year, month) %>%
  summarise(
    arr_flights = sum(arr_flights, na.rm = TRUE),
    not_on_time = sum(arr_del15+ arr_cancelled +    arr_diverted, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(on_time_pr= (arr_flights-not_on_time)/arr_flights) %>% 
  left_join(all_stock, join_by(carrier== Carrier, year== year, month== month), relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
  filter(S_Date>= "2002-01-01" & S_Date<= "2025-11-01") %>% 
  mutate(grow= (close-open)/open,
         grow_log= close/open,
         post_corona= (year>= 2020 & month>= 3) | year>= 2021,
         decade= (year-2000) %/% 10)
model_on_time_fixest <- feols(
  log(grow_log) ~ on_time_pr | carrier + month,
  data = av_delay_df_on_time,
  cluster = ~ carrier
)

summary(model_on_time_fixest)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(grow_log)
## Observations: 1,809
## Fixed-effects: carrier: 8,  month: 12
## Standard-errors: Clustered (carrier) 
##            Estimate Std. Error t value  Pr(>|t|)    
## on_time_pr 0.205751   0.046146 4.45872 0.0029406 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.145493     Adj. R2: 0.027177
##                  Within R2: 0.00836

Add Year Effect

model_stock_fixest_ym <- feols(
  log(grow_log) ~ av_delay | carrier + month+ year,
  data = av_delay_df,
  cluster = ~ carrier
)

summary(model_stock_fixest_ym)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(grow_log)
## Observations: 1,809
## Fixed-effects: carrier: 8,  month: 12,  year: 23
## Standard-errors: Clustered (carrier) 
##           Estimate Std. Error  t value Pr(>|t|)    
## av_delay -0.002723   0.001027 -2.65232 0.032831 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.143697     Adj. R2: 0.039229
##                  Within R2: 0.0047

Lag Model

lag_data <- av_delay_df %>%
  arrange(carrier, S_Date) %>%
  group_by(carrier) %>%
  mutate(
    delay_lag1 = lag(av_delay, 1),
    delay_lag2 = lag(av_delay, 2),
    delay_lag3 = lag(av_delay, 3)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  drop_na(delay_lag1, delay_lag2, delay_lag3)

lag_on_time <- av_delay_df_on_time %>%
  arrange(carrier, S_Date) %>%
  group_by(carrier) %>%
  mutate(
    time_lag1 = lag(on_time_pr, 1),
    time_lag2 = lag(on_time_pr, 2),
    time_lag3 = lag(on_time_pr, 3)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  drop_na(time_lag1, time_lag2, time_lag3)


model_stock_lag3 <- feols(
  log(grow_log) ~ av_delay + delay_lag1 + delay_lag2 + delay_lag3|
                  month + carrier,
  data   = lag_data,
  cluster = ~ carrier
)
 
summary(model_stock_lag3)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: log(grow_log)
## Observations: 1,785
## Fixed-effects: month: 12,  carrier: 8
## Standard-errors: Clustered (carrier) 
##             Estimate Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|)    
## av_delay   -0.000718   0.001463 -0.490731  0.63863    
## delay_lag1 -0.000172   0.001294 -0.133117  0.89785    
## delay_lag2 -0.000159   0.000929 -0.171218  0.86890    
## delay_lag3 -0.002298   0.000947 -2.426414  0.04566 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.144967     Adj. R2: 0.022721
##                  Within R2: 0.007539

Compare all models

models<- list(model_stock_fixest,model_fuel_fixest,model_on_time_fixest,model_stock_fixest_ym,model_stock_lag3)
names(models)<-   c("fixest_C_M", "fixest_C_M+Fuel", "fixest_C_M on time", "fixest_C_M_Y", "fixest_C_M lag3") 
modelsummary::modelsummary(models, title = "model 2-6 values")
model 2-6 values
fixest_C_M fixest_C_M+Fuel fixest_C_M on time fixest_C_M_Y fixest_C_M lag3

Num.Obs. 1809 1809 1809 1809 1785
R2 0.033 0.033 0.037 0.061 0.035
R2 Adj. 0.023 0.022 0.027 0.039 0.023
R2 Within 0.004 0.004 0.008 0.005 0.008
R2 Within Adj. 0.003 0.003 0.008 0.004 0.005
AIC -1792.0 -1790.0 -1800.4 -1801.4 -1783.0
BIC -1682.0 -1674.5 -1690.4 -1570.4 -1656.8
RMSE 0.15 0.15 0.15 0.14 0.14
FE: carrier X X X X X
FE: month X X X X X
FE: year X

first, lets compare the models with a filtered data, that omit the first 3 rows of each carrier in order to compare equal observation comparing .

model_stock_fixest_rl  <- feols(log(grow_log) ~ av_delay | carrier + month,
                                data = lag_data, cluster = ~carrier)

model_fuel_fixest_rl    <- feols(
  log(grow_log) ~ av_delay + fuel_del| carrier + month,
  data = lag_data,
  cluster = ~ carrier
)

model_on_time_fixest_rl <- feols( log(grow_log) ~ on_time_pr | carrier + month,
  data = lag_on_time, cluster = ~ carrier)

model_stock_fixest_ym_rl <- feols(
  log(grow_log) ~ av_delay | carrier + month+ year,
  data = lag_data,
  cluster = ~ carrier
)
models<- list(model_stock_fixest_rl,model_fuel_fixest_rl,model_on_time_fixest_rl,model_stock_fixest_ym_rl,model_stock_lag3)
names(models)<-   c("fixest_C_M", "fixest_C_M+Fuel", "fixest_C_M on time", "fixest_C_M_Y", "fixest_C_M lag3") 
cat("\n--- Model Comparison (equal sample) ---\n")
## 
## --- Model Comparison (equal sample) ---
modelsummary::modelsummary(models, title = "models 2-6 lag compare")
models 2-6 lag compare
fixest_C_M fixest_C_M+Fuel fixest_C_M on time fixest_C_M_Y fixest_C_M lag3

(0.030)

(0.050)

(0.001)

(0.001)

(0.001)
Num.Obs. 1785 1785 1785 1785 1785

R2 Adj. 0.020 0.020 0.025 0.040 0.023

FE: carrier X X X X X
FE: month X X X X X
FE: year X

Using the \(AIC\) model, the Fixed effect model by carrier, year and month looks to be the better one.

the final model (without lag obsservation omiting)

summary(model_stock_fixest_ym)
